headline: Argus Upgrades Nokia to Buy: Can AI Network Demand Wake Up the Sleeping Giant?
summary: Analysts at Argus see a compelling opportunity in Nokia (NYSE:NOK), upgrading the Finnish networking giant’s shares to Buy from Hold with a $15 price target. The call followed Nokia’s Q1 report and points to accelerating AI-driven demand in the Network Infrastructure business. The thesis frames Nokia stock as a potential AI infrastructure dark horse, with ... Argus Upgrades Nokia to Buy: Can AI Network Demand Wake Up the Sleeping Giant?

headline: Cisco Ties Qmulos To Splunk To Deepen Software And Security Reach
summary: Qmulos compliance and analytics solutions were added to Cisco's SolutionsPlus Partner Program, making them available on the Cisco Global Price List. Q-Compliance and Q-BA2 are now sold directly through Cisco channels, with deeper integration into the Splunk platform that Cisco owns. The move expands the NasdaqGS:CSCO software and security ecosystem with automated compliance capabilities for customers and partners. headline: Cisco, Amazon And A Financial Stock On CNBC's 'Final Trades'
